Title: Define Level 1 FCI scope and system boundary
Supporting Owners: Joshua Hornback; Andy Volk; Sulaiman Ahmad
Source Notes: Use the Level 1 Foundation and Scope sections plus the current gap register.
Description: Write the actual Level 1 scope statement for the organization. Identify which systems, users, devices, locations, and business processes handle FCI. Note anything explicitly out of scope so later evidence work stays bounded.
Definition of Done: A written scope statement exists, identifies the in-scope FCI environment, names the main systems and locations, and is approved for the rest of the Level 1 work.

Title: Assign Level 1 control and evidence owners
Supporting Owners: Ryan Lin; Joshua Hornback; Sulaiman Ahmad
Source Notes: Use the owner matrix file and team roles already defined in the project plan.
Description: Update the owner matrix so each Level 1 family and each required artifact has one accountable owner and any needed support owners. Resolve any gaps where ownership is implied but not explicit.
Definition of Done: The owner matrix names an accountable owner for every Level 1 family, artifact, and evidence set, and the team has agreed to the assignments.

Title: Build the Level 1 evidence index
Source Notes: Use the evidence index template and the Level 1 task list from the gap review.
Description: For each of the 17 Level 1 practices, record the source artifact, the operational proof to collect, the owner, and where the evidence will be stored. Use the existing Level 1 files as the starting inventory.
Definition of Done: All 17 Level 1 practices are listed with artifact references, evidence expectations, owners, and storage locations or a clear gap note.

Title: Run the Level 1 gap assessment
Source Notes: Use the current tracker and compare it against the evidence index and Level 1 gap review.
Description: Review each Level 1 practice and mark it as covered, partially covered, or missing. Capture the specific missing artifact, missing implementation proof, or missing approval needed to close the practice.
Definition of Done: The tracker shows current status for all Level 1 practices, with gaps described in plain language and assigned to an owner.

Title: Assemble the Level 1 readiness package
Source Notes: Use the evidence index, the gap tracker, and the Level 1 readiness expectations in the project plan.
Description: Create the reviewable package for leadership and internal readiness review. Include the scope statement, owner matrix, evidence index, open gaps, and a short summary of what is ready versus what still needs work.
Definition of Done: Leadership can open one package and understand scope, evidence status, and remaining Level 1 gaps without reading the entire repository.

Title: Tailor Level 1 AC policies and role records
Source Notes: Use the Level 1 AC practice sections and the existing AC templates.
Description: Replace template language in the Level 1 Access Control files with the real environment. Define authorized users and devices, approved roles, how access is enforced, and which systems and external services are allowed.
Definition of Done: The AC policy and related records reflect the real environment, identify authorized access rules, and are usable as evidence without further interpretation.

Title: Validate Level 1 external access and public posting controls
Source Notes: Use the external access and public posting files in Level 1 AC.
Description: Document and validate how remote access, VPN use, third-party or external systems, and public posting review are handled today. Update the approved external systems list and content review records so they represent current practice.
Definition of Done: External access rules, approved systems, VPN details, and public posting controls are current and supported by records that can be shown during review.

Title: Collect Level 1 AC evidence
Source Notes: Use the AC inventories plus the Level 1 evidence index.
Description: Collect the actual evidence for Level 1 AC: current user and device lists, authorized personnel lists, approval records, and any system settings or screenshots needed to prove enforcement. Link each evidence item back to the Level 1 evidence index.
Definition of Done: Each Level 1 AC practice has current evidence attached or referenced, and the evidence index shows exactly where reviewers can find it.

Title: Tailor Level 1 IA documentation
Source Notes: Use the Level 1 IA practice sections and existing IA files.
Description: Update the Level 1 identification and authentication documents so they describe the actual account lifecycle, authentication methods, and password requirements in use. Call out how unique identities are assigned and how passwords are created, reset, and protected.
Definition of Done: The IA documents describe real operating practice and can be used by the owner to explain how identity and authentication controls work in the environment.

Title: Validate Level 1 account inventory and authentication settings
Source Notes: Use the account inventory file and the evidence index.
Description: Update the system account and device identifier inventory, then confirm that the listed accounts align to the actual systems in scope. Validate that authentication settings and password rules match the documented process.
Definition of Done: The account inventory is current, authentication settings are verified, and any mismatches are captured as gaps with owners.

Title: Tailor Level 1 media handling documents
Source Notes: Use the Level 1 MP practice section and existing MP files.
Description: Rewrite the media disposal and reuse documents so they describe the organization’s actual media handling process, including when media is sanitized, destroyed, reused, or retained.
Definition of Done: The media policy and reuse procedure describe the real process and clearly state what staff must do with media that may contain FCI.

Title: Collect Level 1 media disposal and sanitization evidence
Source Notes: Use the disposal log template and the evidence index entry for MP.L1-3.8.3.
Description: Populate or revise the disposal log format so it can be used as operational evidence, then collect any existing sanitization or destruction records for in-scope media.
Definition of Done: There is a usable disposal log format plus current records or a documented operational gap with an owner and closure plan.

Title: Tailor Level 1 physical access and visitor policies
Source Notes: Use the Level 1 PE sections and the existing PE policies.
Description: Update the physical access and visitor management policies to describe the real office, facility, or worksite controls in use. Include who is allowed access, how visitors are handled, how badges or other access methods are issued, and how the site is protected.
Definition of Done: The physical access and visitor policies accurately describe the real site controls and can be followed by staff without extra explanation.

Title: Update Level 1 access lists, badge records, and visitor logs
Source Notes: Use the existing PE record files.
Description: Review and update the authorized access lists, badge issuance records, visitor or access logs, and physical access device inventory so they match the current environment.
Definition of Done: The PE records are current, complete for in-scope personnel and sites, and ready to be cited as evidence.

Title: Update Level 1 network protection documents and diagrams
Source Notes: Use the Level 1 SC sections and the current SC files.
Description: Update the network monitoring, segmentation, and network diagram files so they reflect the current environment that handles FCI. Make sure the diagrams and text show the real boundary protections and any public-facing separation.
Definition of Done: The SC documents and diagrams match the live environment and clearly show how the FCI boundary is protected.

Title: Validate Level 1 firewall and boundary controls
Source Notes: Use the firewall and IDS file plus the Level 1 evidence index.
Description: Review the actual firewall, IDS, monitoring, and segmentation controls in place for the in-scope environment. Update the record to show what is actually configured and collect the proof needed to demonstrate boundary protection.
Definition of Done: The documented boundary protections match the implemented controls, and evidence is collected for the applicable SC Level 1 practices.

Title: Update Level 1 patch and vulnerability documents
Source Notes: Use the Level 1 SI sections and existing SI patching files.
Description: Update the patch management policy and vulnerability scan reporting format so they describe the real tools, cadence, and remediation process used in the environment.
Definition of Done: The patch and vulnerability documents describe the actual operating model and can be used to guide recurring evidence collection.

Title: Update Level 1 malware protection documents
Source Notes: Use the SI antivirus policy, update policy, and configuration file.
Description: Update the malware protection, signature update, and antivirus configuration files so they match the actual toolset and operating settings in place.
Definition of Done: The antivirus and update documentation reflects the live environment and names the real operating expectations.

Title: Collect Level 1 scanning, updates, and remediation evidence
Source Notes: Use the SI log templates and the evidence index.
Description: Collect or update the actual scan logs, update logs, vulnerability results, and remediation records needed to prove periodic scanning, malicious code protection, and flaw remediation are happening in practice.
Definition of Done: Current SI operating evidence exists for patching, malware protection, updates, and scanning, or the gaps are clearly logged for remediation.

Title: Define the Level 2 CUI boundary
Supporting Owners: Joshua Hornback; Andy Volk; Sulaiman Ahmad
Source Notes: Use the SSP, Level 2 plan, and Level 2 gap review.
Description: Write the actual CUI scope statement. Identify the in-scope people, systems, services, locations, external providers, and data flows. Call out any assets that are out of scope or treated specially.
Definition of Done: The organization has an approved Level 2 scope statement that can be used to justify the assessment boundary and drive the SSP update.

Title: Tailor the Level 2 SSP to the real environment
Supporting Owners: Andy Volk; Ryan Lin; Sulaiman Ahmad
Source Notes: Use the SSP file and the handbook sections on scoping and assessment evidence.
Description: Replace placeholder text in the SSP with real system names, architecture details, asset inventory, trust zones, data flow descriptions, inherited services, and owner information. Remove references that do not apply.
Definition of Done: The SSP reads like the organization’s real environment, not a template, and can be used as the primary reference for Level 2 readiness.

Title: Assign Level 2 domain and evidence owners
Supporting Owners: Ryan Lin; Joshua Hornback; Sulaiman Ahmad
Source Notes: Use the project plan team roles and the current Level 2 gap register.
Description: Define one accountable owner and any support owners for each Level 2 domain, the SSP, the evidence index, the POA&M, and the mock assessment process.
Definition of Done: Every Level 2 domain and readiness artifact has a named accountable owner and support model.

Title: Build the Level 2 evidence index
Source Notes: Use the Level 2 evidence index template and the SSP practice mappings.
Description: Create a practice-to-evidence map for Level 2 that points each practice to the intended policy, procedure, technical proof, operational record, and owner. Use the existing family files and the SSP mappings as the starting structure.
Definition of Done: The evidence index covers the Level 2 practices in scope and clearly tells reviewers where each artifact or proof item should come from.

Title: Stand up the Level 2 POA&M and gap tracker
Supporting Owners: Joshua Hornback; Ryan Lin; Sulaiman Ahmad
Source Notes: Use the POA&M tracker, gap register, and Level 2 gap review.
Description: Convert the current gap picture into a usable remediation tracker. Record missing documents, missing evidence, implementation gaps, owners, target dates, and the expected closure artifact for each item.
Definition of Done: There is one working Level 2 remediation tracker that the team can use to drive execution and report status.

Title: Prepare the Level 2 mock assessment plan
Supporting Owners: Joshua Hornback; Sulaiman Ahmad; Ryan Lin
Source Notes: Use the mock assessment checklist and the readiness milestones from the project plan.
Description: Turn the mock assessment checklist into an execution plan. Define who will present which domains, how evidence will be shown, what still blocks a useful mock assessment, and what pre-work must be completed first.
Definition of Done: The team has a scheduled, owner-based mock assessment plan with clear prerequisites and logistics.

Title: Run the internal Level 2 mock assessment
Supporting Owners: Andy Volk; Ryan Lin; Sulaiman Ahmad
Source Notes: Use the checklist, evidence index, and SSP as the assessment package.
Description: Conduct the mock assessment, record which practices were demonstrably met, and capture every missing artifact, weak answer, or evidence problem into the POA&M.
Definition of Done: The mock assessment is complete, findings are logged, and the team has a realistic readiness picture instead of an assumed one.

Title: Prepare the executive Level 2 readiness summary
Source Notes: Use the project plan, POA&M, and gap register.
Description: Prepare the leadership view of Level 2 readiness. Summarize scope, strongest areas, highest-risk gaps, remaining work by owner, and the decision points that need executive approval or resource support.
Definition of Done: Leadership can review one summary and understand the true Level 2 readiness posture, risks, and next decisions.

Title: Create and tailor the Level 2 AC policy and procedure set
Source Notes: AC is called out as missing from the Level 2 folder. Use the SSP mappings for 3.1.1 through 3.1.22 and the handbook AC sections.
Description: Create the Level 2 Access Control policy and procedure set for the live CUI environment. Cover access authorization, CUI flow, privilege management, session controls, remote access, mobile and wireless controls, external systems, and public information handling.
Definition of Done: There is a usable AC policy and procedure set that maps to the AC Level 2 practices and describes the real operating environment.

Title: Validate Level 2 AC implementation
Source Notes: Use the SSP control mapping and the handbook AC examples such as remote access and notices.
Description: Review the actual environment for least privilege, privileged access, failed logon handling, session lock and timeout, remote access protections, external system use, and mobile or wireless controls. Record any control gaps that prevent the documentation from being true.
Definition of Done: The AC control set is validated against the real environment, and any mismatches are either fixed or recorded in the POA&M.

Title: Collect Level 2 AC evidence
Source Notes: Use the Level 2 evidence index and the SSP mappings for the AC family.
Description: Collect the actual proof for the AC family: account and role records, approval records, remote access settings, banners, session settings, and any system exports or screenshots needed to support review.
Definition of Done: The AC family has an evidence set that is current, organized, and mapped to the Level 2 evidence index.

Title: Create and tailor the Level 2 IA policy and procedure set
Source Notes: IA is called out as missing from the Level 2 folder. Use the SSP mappings for 3.5.1 through 3.5.11 and the handbook IA examples.
Description: Create the Level 2 IA policy and procedure set covering unique IDs, MFA, password controls, temporary passwords, identifier management, feedback masking, and authentication requirements for the real environment.
Definition of Done: There is a usable IA policy and procedure set mapped to the Level 2 IA practices and tailored to the live environment.

Title: Validate Level 2 IA controls
Source Notes: Use the SSP IA mappings and handbook IA guidance, especially MFA.
Description: Check the actual systems to confirm MFA coverage, password policy settings, account provisioning and deprovisioning, identifier handling, and authentication methods match the documented control set.
Definition of Done: The IA controls are verified against the live environment, and any gaps are documented with enough detail to remediate them.

Title: Collect Level 2 IA evidence
Source Notes: Use the Level 2 evidence index and SSP practice mappings for IA.
Description: Collect the account inventories, policy references, screenshots, exports, and other proof needed to demonstrate the identity and authentication controls in practice.
Definition of Done: The IA family evidence set is current, organized, and mapped in the evidence index.

Title: Tailor the Level 2 AU policy and procedure set
Source Notes: Use the existing AU files and SSP mappings for 3.3.1 through 3.3.9.
Description: Update the AU policy and procedure files to match the actual logging systems, retention approach, alerting process, time synchronization source, and review cadence in use. Remove any Level 3 or non-applicable text.
Definition of Done: The AU documentation reflects the real audit and logging environment and can be used directly during readiness review.

Title: Validate Level 2 logging and audit review operations
Source Notes: Use the AU procedure and the SSP mappings.
Description: Confirm that the logging platform, audit protection, review process, alerting behavior, reporting, and time source controls are actually operating as documented.
Definition of Done: The AU implementation is validated against the real environment, and any control gaps are recorded in the POA&M.

Title: Collect Level 2 AU evidence
Source Notes: Use the evidence index and the AU family files.
Description: Collect the operational proof for the AU family, including sample audit reviews, retention settings, alert records, and system configuration references that support the documented control set.
Definition of Done: The AU evidence set is complete enough for an internal reviewer to trace the family from policy to operational proof.

Title: Tailor the Level 2 AT policy and procedure
Source Notes: Use the existing AT files and the handbook sections on awareness and training.
Description: Update the awareness and training documents so they describe the actual training model, role-based expectations, insider threat coverage, and cadence used by the organization.
Definition of Done: The AT documents describe the real training program and clearly show what training is required, for whom, and how completion is tracked.

Title: Validate Level 2 training completion and insider threat coverage
Source Notes: Use the AT procedure and the evidence index.
Description: Confirm that the defined training actually occurs, that role-based personnel are included, and that insider threat awareness is covered in a way that can be demonstrated.
Definition of Done: The AT program is validated with real completion records or a clearly defined remediation plan for missing coverage.

Title: Tailor the Level 2 CM policy and procedure set
Source Notes: Use the CM files and the SSP mappings for 3.4.1 through 3.4.9.
Description: Update the configuration management policy and procedure to match the real baselines, change control process, security impact review, least functionality approach, and software control model used in the environment.
Definition of Done: The CM documentation reflects the actual configuration and change management process used by the organization.

Title: Validate Level 2 baselines, change control, and software restrictions
Source Notes: Use the CM procedure and SSP mappings.
Description: Review how systems are baselined, how changes are approved, how security impact is considered, and how nonessential or user-installed software is restricted.
Definition of Done: The CM controls are validated against the actual operating environment, and any gaps are recorded for remediation.

Title: Collect Level 2 CM evidence
Source Notes: Use the evidence index and the CM files.
Description: Collect the baseline references, change records, approvals, and other operational proof needed to demonstrate the CM family during internal review.
Definition of Done: The CM evidence set is organized, current, and traceable to the applicable Level 2 practices.

Title: Create the missing Level 2 IR policy and align it to the procedure
Source Notes: IR policy is called out as missing. Use the existing IR procedure and the handbook IR sections.
Description: Create the missing Incident Response policy and make sure it aligns with the current incident response procedure, roles, escalation path, testing expectations, and evidence handling approach.
Definition of Done: The organization has both an IR policy and procedure that align to each other and describe the real operating process.

Title: Validate Level 2 incident reporting, handling, and testing workflow
Source Notes: Use the IR procedure and SSP mappings for 3.6.1 through 3.6.3.
Description: Confirm that incidents can be reported, escalated, investigated, and tested as documented. Check whether the organization can produce realistic records of incident handling and testing if asked.
Definition of Done: The IR workflow is validated against real operations, and any missing steps or evidence are added to the POA&M.

Title: Collect Level 2 IR evidence
Source Notes: Use the evidence index and the IR procedure.
Description: Collect the artifacts needed to show incident handling, reporting, and testing, such as escalation records, exercise notes, response checklists, or sample logs.
Definition of Done: The IR evidence set is current enough to support a walkthrough of the incident response process.

Title: Tailor the Level 2 maintenance policy and procedure
Source Notes: Use the MA files and SSP mappings for 3.7.1 through 3.7.6.
Description: Update the maintenance documents to describe the real maintenance process, including remote maintenance, sanitation, inspection, and who is authorized to perform work on in-scope systems.
Definition of Done: The MA policy and procedure reflect the real maintenance model used by the organization.

Title: Validate Level 2 maintenance controls and collect support records
Source Notes: Use the MA procedure and evidence index.
Description: Confirm that maintenance is controlled as documented and collect the records or examples that show maintenance personnel, remote maintenance, and media inspection controls can be demonstrated.
Definition of Done: The MA controls are validated and the supporting records are mapped into the evidence index.

Title: Create and tailor the Level 2 MP policy and procedure set
Source Notes: MP is called out as missing from the Level 2 folder. Use the SSP mappings for 3.8.1 through 3.8.9.
Description: Create the Media Protection policy and procedure set for the live CUI environment. Cover media access, disposal, marking, accountability, removable media, encryption, shared media, and backup protection.
Definition of Done: There is a usable MP policy and procedure set mapped to the Level 2 MP practices and tailored to the actual environment.

Title: Validate Level 2 media handling, disposal, and backup controls
Source Notes: Use the SSP MP mappings and the handbook media protection guidance.
Description: Check how media is actually handled today, including disposal, transport, marking, removable media use, and backup protection. Record any operating gaps between practice and documentation.
Definition of Done: The MP controls are validated against real operations, and the key evidence and gaps are documented.

Title: Collect Level 2 MP evidence
Source Notes: Use the evidence index and the Level 2 MP practice mappings.
Description: Collect the operational records needed to support the MP family, such as disposal records, media inventories, handling logs, or backup protection evidence.
Definition of Done: The MP evidence set is current, organized, and mapped into the evidence index.

Title: Tailor the Level 2 personnel security policy and procedure
Source Notes: Use the existing PS files and the handbook personnel security sections.
Description: Update the personnel security documents to describe how screening and personnel actions are really handled for staff and contractors with in-scope access.
Definition of Done: The PS policy and procedure reflect the actual screening and personnel action process used by the organization.

Title: Validate Level 2 screening and personnel action controls
Source Notes: Use the PS procedure and evidence index.
Description: Confirm that screening and personnel action steps can be demonstrated with real records or with a clear documented operating process that is actually followed.
Definition of Done: The PS family can be explained and evidenced through current records or an explicit remediation plan.

Title: Create and tailor the Level 2 PE policy and procedure set
Source Notes: PE is called out as missing from the Level 2 folder. Use the SSP mappings for 3.10.1 through 3.10.6.
Description: Create the Physical Protection policy and procedure set for the live environment. Cover facility monitoring, visitor escorting, logs, physical access management, and alternative work sites.
Definition of Done: There is a usable PE policy and procedure set mapped to the Level 2 PE practices and tailored to the actual sites and work model.

Title: Validate Level 2 facility access, visitor, and alternate work site controls
Source Notes: Use the SSP PE mappings and the handbook physical protection guidance.
Description: Check that the real facility access controls, visitor handling, logs, and alternate work site rules match what the documentation says. Identify any missing logs, missing approvals, or weak practices.
Definition of Done: The PE controls are validated against the real environment, and the family gaps are recorded clearly enough to remediate.

Title: Collect Level 2 PE evidence
Source Notes: Use the evidence index and the Level 2 PE mappings.
Description: Collect the records and proof needed for the PE family, such as access lists, visitor logs, monitoring records, and alternate site control records if applicable.
Definition of Done: The PE evidence set is current and mapped into the evidence index in a way an internal reviewer can follow.

Title: Tailor the Level 2 risk assessment policy and procedure
Source Notes: Use the RA files and SSP mappings for 3.11.1 through 3.11.3.
Description: Update the risk assessment documents to describe the real cadence, vulnerability scanning approach, remediation workflow, and who owns decisions on risk acceptance or closure.
Definition of Done: The RA documentation reflects the actual risk and vulnerability management process used by the organization.

Title: Validate Level 2 scanning cadence and remediation workflow
Source Notes: Use the RA procedure and the POA&M tracker.
Description: Confirm that vulnerability scans happen with a defined cadence, that results are reviewed, and that remediation actions are tracked to closure.
Definition of Done: The RA controls are validated with real scanning and remediation records or the gaps are clearly documented in the POA&M.

Title: Collect Level 2 RA evidence
Source Notes: Use the evidence index and RA family files.
Description: Collect the scan outputs, review notes, and remediation records needed to support the risk assessment family during readiness review.
Definition of Done: The RA evidence set is organized, current, and traceable to the applicable practices.

Title: Tailor the Level 2 security assessment policy and procedure
Source Notes: Use the CA files and SSP mappings for 3.12.1 through 3.12.4.
Description: Update the security assessment documents so they describe the organization’s real assessment cadence, monitoring approach, POA&M handling, and SSP maintenance model.
Definition of Done: The CA documentation reflects the actual internal assessment and monitoring process used by the organization.

Title: Validate Level 2 assessment, monitoring, and POA&M use
Source Notes: Use the CA procedure, POA&M tracker, and SSP.
Description: Confirm that the organization can show recurring assessment activity, tracked deficiencies, control monitoring, and active SSP maintenance rather than one-time documentation.
Definition of Done: The CA family is supported by an operating assessment process, not only written documents.

Title: Collect Level 2 CA evidence
Source Notes: Use the evidence index and CA family files.
Description: Collect the records that demonstrate the security assessment program is operating, including internal reviews, POA&M updates, and SSP maintenance evidence.
Definition of Done: The CA evidence set is current, organized, and ready for mock assessment walkthrough.

Title: Create and tailor the Level 2 SC policy and procedure set
Source Notes: SC is called out as missing from the Level 2 folder. Use the SSP mappings for 3.13.1 through 3.13.16.
Description: Create the System and Communications Protection policy and procedure set for the real environment. Cover boundary protection, segmentation, encryption, remote paths, key management, data in transit and at rest, split tunneling, and communications authenticity.
Definition of Done: There is a usable SC policy and procedure set mapped to the Level 2 SC practices and tailored to the live environment.

Title: Validate Level 2 boundary, encryption, and remote communication controls
Source Notes: Use the SSP SC mappings and handbook examples for communications protection.
Description: Review the actual environment for segmentation, boundary controls, encryption use, remote administration paths, split tunneling, public system separation, and communication integrity.
Definition of Done: The SC controls are validated against the live environment, and implementation gaps are either fixed or recorded in the POA&M.

Title: Collect Level 2 SC evidence
Source Notes: Use the evidence index and the SSP architecture content.
Description: Collect the network diagrams, firewall or segmentation references, encryption settings, and other proof needed to support the SC family during internal review.
Definition of Done: The SC evidence set is current, organized, and mapped to the evidence index.

Title: Create and tailor the Level 2 SI policy and procedure set
Source Notes: SI is called out as missing from the Level 2 folder. Use the SSP mappings for 3.14.1 through 3.14.7.
Description: Create the System and Information Integrity policy and procedure set for the real environment. Cover flaw remediation, malicious code protection, alerts and advisories, periodic scans, communications monitoring, and unauthorized use detection.
Definition of Done: There is a usable SI policy and procedure set mapped to the Level 2 SI practices and tailored to the live environment.

Title: Validate Level 2 patching, scanning, alerting, and misuse detection
Source Notes: Use the SSP SI mappings and handbook SI guidance.
Description: Review the actual patching, antivirus, monitoring, and scanning processes in the environment to confirm the documented control set is true in practice.
Definition of Done: The SI controls are validated with current operational proof, and any weak areas are captured in the POA&M.

Title: Collect Level 2 SI evidence
Source Notes: Use the evidence index and the SSP SI mappings.
Description: Collect the scan outputs, patch records, alert handling examples, monitoring records, and other proof needed to support the SI family during readiness review.
Definition of Done: The SI evidence set is current, organized, and traceable to the applicable Level 2 practices.
